Common_Causes: Boost leak in charge air pipes; faulty waste gate; worn supercharger rotors; regulating flap stuck open; N75 solenoid fault
Symptoms: Loss of power especially below 3500rpm; poor acceleration; no boost gauge movement
Recommended_Fix: Check boost pipes for leaks; test N75 solenoid valve; inspect regulating flap J808; check waste gate operation

Common_Causes: N75 solenoid valve stuck closed; waste gate actuator failed; charge pressure control hose disconnected
Symptoms: Engine enters limp mode; over-revving; boost gauge pegged; possible detonation
Recommended_Fix: Inspect N75 valve and pressure canister; check waste gate hose and actuator diaphragm

Description: Charge Air Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
Common_Causes: Damaged sensor; chafed wiring; intake air leak before sensor; sensor contamination
Symptoms: Incorrect boost regulation; possible limp mode; poor throttle response
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring to G31; compare G31 reading with G583 at idle; replace sensor if readings implausible

Description: Charge Air Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Input
Common_Causes: Short to ground on signal wire; failed sensor; damaged connector
Symptoms: No boost pressure reading; turbocharger runs unregulated; possible overboost
Recommended_Fix: Inspect connector and wiring harness to G31; replace sensor

Description: Charge Air Pressure Sensor Circuit High Input
Common_Causes: Open circuit on signal wire; short to positive; failed sensor
Symptoms: No boost pressure reading; engine enters boost-limited mode
Recommended_Fix: Inspect wiring for open circuit; check sensor supply voltage; replace sensor

Description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
Affected_Component: Intake Manifold Pressure Sender G71 with G42
Common_Causes: Vacuum leak at intake manifold; blocked sensor port; wiring fault; failed sensor
Symptoms: Rough idle; poor fuel trim; incorrect air mass calculation; supercharger boost control fault
Recommended_Fix: Check intake manifold for vacuum leaks; clean sensor port; verify wiring; replace G71 if confirmed faulty

Description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Input
Affected_Component: Intake Manifold Pressure Sender G71 with G42
Common_Causes: Short to ground on signal wire; sensor failure
Symptoms: Incorrect MAP reading; engine may run rich; poor idle quality
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for short to ground; replace G71/G42 combined sender

Description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Circuit High Input
Affected_Component: Intake Manifold Pressure Sender G71 with G42
Common_Causes: Open circuit on signal wire; short to positive; sensor failure
Symptoms: Incorrect MAP reading; supercharger boost regulation failure
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for open circuit; replace G71/G42 combined sender

Description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance (Supercharger)
Affected_Component: Intake Air Temperature Sender G520 (with G583)
Common_Causes: Sensor exposed to excessive heat; wiring damage near supercharger
Symptoms: Supercharger power may be unnecessarily throttled (protective limit is 130°C); incorrect boost regulation
Recommended_Fix: Inspect wiring routing near supercharger for heat damage; replace G583/G520 sender

Description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Low (Supercharger side)
Affected_Component: Intake Air Temperature Sender G42 (with G71)
Common_Causes: Short to ground; failed sensor
Symptoms: Incorrect IAT reading used for boost correction; possible rich fuelling
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring; replace combined G71/G42 sender

Description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit High (Supercharger side)
Affected_Component: Intake Air Temperature Sender G42 (with G71)
Common_Causes: Open circuit; failed sensor
Symptoms: Incorrect IAT reading; possible lean fuelling at cold start
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for open circuit; replace combined G71/G42 sender

Description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
Common_Causes: Air in fuel system; failing high-pressure fuel pump; wiring fault; blocked fuel strainer
Symptoms: Engine runs rough; loss of power; potential engine stall; drastically reduced torque on failure
Recommended_Fix: Check fuel supply pressure at low-pressure side; inspect high-pressure pump cam and roller tappet; check G247 wiring

Description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Input
Common_Causes: Short to ground on signal circuit; failed sensor; low fuel pressure
Symptoms: Engine runs on reduced torque using low-pressure fallback; rough running
Recommended_Fix: Check G247 wiring; verify low-pressure fuel supply; replace sensor if supply is adequate

Description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit High Input
Common_Causes: Open circuit on signal wire; failed sensor
Symptoms: Fuel pressure regulating valve N276 deactivated; engine runs on electrical pump pressure only
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for open circuit; replace G247

Affected_Component: High-Pressure Fuel Pump / Fuel Pressure Regulating Valve N276
Common_Causes: Worn high-pressure fuel pump; blocked fuel filter; weak electrical fuel pump G6; fuel pressure regulating valve N276 stuck open; low fuel level
Symptoms: Hard start; hesitation under load; lean misfire; low boost pressure visible
Recommended_Fix: Check low-pressure supply (should be 0.5–6.5 bar); inspect N276 valve; check high-pressure pump roller tappet (increased stroke 5.7mm on TSI)

Common_Causes: N276 valve stuck closed; fuel return line blocked
Symptoms: Rough running; fuel smell; possible injector damage; rich mixture
Recommended_Fix: Test N276 valve operation; check fuel return line; replace N276 if stuck

Common_Causes: Damaged sensor tip; metallic debris on sender wheel; wiring fault; damaged crankshaft sealing flange sender wheel
Symptoms: Engine will not start or stops suddenly; no injection or ignition possible
Recommended_Fix: Inspect G28 for damage or contamination; check sender wheel on crankshaft sealing flange; verify wiring

Common_Causes: Intermittent wiring fault; partial tooth damage on sender wheel; excessive air gap
Symptoms: Intermittent misfires; unstable idle; stalling
Recommended_Fix: Inspect sender wheel teeth for damage; check air gap; check wiring for intermittent fault

Common_Causes: Failed Hall sender; wiring fault; damaged inlet camshaft teeth (4 teeth scanned)
Symptoms: Engine may continue running but cannot be restarted; camshaft adjustment disabled; inlet camshaft held in late position; loss of torque
Recommended_Fix: Inspect G40 sensor and wiring; check inlet camshaft for damage to trigger teeth

Common_Causes: Intermittent wiring; sensor partially contaminated; timing chain wear causing out-of-range signal
Symptoms: Intermittent start issues; poor torque especially at low rpm; camshaft adjustment erratic
Recommended_Fix: Check timing chain for wear/stretch; inspect G40 wiring and mounting

Description: Crankshaft/Camshaft Timing Correlation Bank 1 Sensor A
Affected_Component: Inlet Camshaft Vane Adjuster / Hall Sender G40 / Engine Speed Sender G28
Common_Causes: Worn timing chain; failed vane adjuster; blocked oil feed to camshaft adjuster N205; incorrect engine oil
Symptoms: Rough idle; loss of power; rattling on start-up
Recommended_Fix: Check timing chain stretch; verify oil pressure at vane adjuster; inspect N205 valve; check oil quality and level

Affected_Component: Inlet Camshaft Timing Adjustment Valve N205 / Vane Adjuster
Common_Causes: N205 valve stuck open; blocked vane adjuster oil gallery; ECU commanding maximum advance
Symptoms: Poor fuel economy; rough idle; emissions fault; possible rattle
Recommended_Fix: Test N205 valve electrically; check oil supply to vane adjuster; inspect timing chain

Affected_Component: Inlet Camshaft Timing Adjustment Valve N205 / Vane Adjuster
Common_Causes: N205 valve stuck closed (holds late position on failure); oil sludge in vane adjuster
Symptoms: Loss of low-rpm torque; poor idle; N205 default failure mode
Recommended_Fix: Test N205 valve; flush oil system; replace N205 if faulty; check oil condition

Description: Ignition/Distributor Engine Speed Input Circuit Range/Performance
Common_Causes: Intermittent G28 signal; EMI interference; damaged wiring near starter motor
Symptoms: Intermittent misfires; rough running at specific rpm
Recommended_Fix: Check G28 wiring routing for EMI sources; clean connector

Common_Causes: Faulty ignition coil(s); blocked injectors; low fuel pressure; boost leak; knock causing retarded ignition
Symptoms: Rough running; loss of power; increased emissions; catalyst damage possible
Recommended_Fix: Perform cylinder contribution test; check all coils N70/N127/N291/N292; check injectors N30–N33; verify fuel pressure

Common_Causes: Failed ignition coil N70; failed injector N30; low compression; carbon on spark plug
Symptoms: Rough idle; possible catalyst overheating; cylinder deactivated on confirmed fault
Recommended_Fix: Swap coil to another cylinder to confirm; test injector N30; check compression

Common_Causes: Failed ignition coil N127; failed injector N31; low compression
Symptoms: Rough idle; possible catalyst overheating
Recommended_Fix: Swap coil to another cylinder to confirm; test injector N31; check compression

Common_Causes: Failed ignition coil N291; failed injector N32; low compression
Symptoms: Rough idle; possible catalyst overheating
Recommended_Fix: Swap coil to another cylinder to confirm; test injector N32; check compression

Common_Causes: Failed ignition coil N292; failed injector N33; low compression
Symptoms: Rough idle; possible catalyst overheating
Recommended_Fix: Swap coil to another cylinder to confirm; test injector N33; check compression

Common_Causes: Failed sensor; loose mounting bolt (affects sensitivity); chafed wiring; sensor located below supercharger on cylinder block
Symptoms: Ignition angle of all cylinders retarded to fixed late value; increased fuel consumption; loss of power and torque
Recommended_Fix: Check G61 mounting torque; inspect wiring; replace G61

Common_Causes: Short to ground on signal wire; failed sensor
Symptoms: All cylinder ignition timing retarded; increased fuel consumption
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for short; replace G61

Common_Causes: Loose mounting bolt reducing acoustic coupling; sensor sensitivity degraded
Symptoms: Premature detonation possible; loss of power due to over-retarded ignition
Recommended_Fix: Torque G61 to specification; replace if still faulty

Common_Causes: Failed sensor; wiring fault; damaged connector near coolant distributor
Symptoms: Engine management uses mapped temperature substitute; incorrect injection timing; cold start enrichment errors
Recommended_Fix: Check G62 wiring; replace sender

Common_Causes: Short to ground; failed sensor
Symptoms: ECU calculates substitute coolant temperature from map; poor cold start behaviour
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for short to ground; replace G62

Common_Causes: Open circuit; failed sensor
Symptoms: ECU uses substitute temperature value; poor hot-start enrichment
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring for open circuit; replace G62

Description: Coolant Temperature Below Thermostat Regulating Temperature
Common_Causes: Thermostat stuck open; dual-circuit thermostat stage 1 (opens at 80°C) failed; coolant temperature sender G83 faulty
Symptoms: Engine slow to reach operating temperature; heater output poor; increased fuel consumption; cylinder block coolant circuit fails to hold 95°C
Recommended_Fix: Replace thermostat 1 if stuck open; check G83 sender

Description: Radiator Outlet Coolant Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
Affected_Component: Radiator Outlet Coolant Temperature Sender G83
Common_Causes: Failed sensor; G62 used as substitute; slow radiator fan response
Symptoms: Incorrect radiator fan control; potential overheating risk
Recommended_Fix: Replace G83; compare readings with G62

Description: Lambda Probe Heater Circuit Low (Before Catalyst)
Common_Causes: Short to ground in heater circuit; failed heater element in probe; blown fuse
Symptoms: Slow lambda probe warm-up; delayed closed-loop control; emissions fault on cold start
Recommended_Fix: Check Z19 heater resistance; inspect wiring and fuse; replace G39 assembly if heater failed

Description: Lambda Probe Heater Circuit High (Before Catalyst)
Common_Causes: Open circuit in heater circuit; failed relay
Symptoms: Slow closed-loop entry; cold start emissions high
Recommended_Fix: Check heater circuit wiring; replace G39/Z19 assembly

Common_Causes: Failed step-type lambda probe; wiring fault; probe contaminated by oil or coolant
Symptoms: No Lambda control; injection quantity runs on pre-control only; Lambda adjustment blocked; activated charcoal filter in emergency mode
Recommended_Fix: Replace G39; check for oil or coolant contamination in exhaust

Description: Lambda Probe Circuit Slow Response (Before Catalyst)
Common_Causes: Aged probe with slow switching response; slight contamination; wiring resistance high
Symptoms: Emissions increase; fuel trim corrections slow; slight rich/lean oscillation
Recommended_Fix: Replace G39 probe

Description: Lambda Probe Heater Circuit Low (After Catalyst)
Common_Causes: Short to ground in heater circuit; failed heater element; blown fuse
Symptoms: Catalyst monitoring delayed or disabled; no MIL for catalyst efficiency
Recommended_Fix: Check Z29 heater resistance; inspect wiring; replace G130/Z29 assembly

Common_Causes: Failed probe; wiring fault
Symptoms: Catalyst efficiency monitoring disabled
Recommended_Fix: Replace G130; inspect wiring

Affected_Component: Three-Way Catalytic Converter / Lambda Probe G130
Common_Causes: Aged catalyst; misfires causing catalyst damage; oil or coolant contamination; exhaust leak before catalyst
Symptoms: MIL illuminated; increased emissions; failed emissions test
Recommended_Fix: Check for misfires first; inspect exhaust manifold and connecting pipe (air-gap insulated on TSI); replace catalyst if confirmed degraded

Affected_Component: Engine Control Unit J623 / Lambda System
Common_Causes: Large boost leak; faulty G71 MAP sensor; failed G39 lambda probe; injector fault; large crankcase ventilation leak
Symptoms: Rough running; failed emissions test; poor fuel economy
Recommended_Fix: Check for intake air leaks especially after supercharger; inspect crankcase ventilation hoses; check lambda probe operation

Common_Causes: Low fuel pressure; boost leak in intercooler circuit; blocked injector; MAF/MAP error
Symptoms: Poor performance; lean hesitation; high idle
Recommended_Fix: Check all charge air hose connections post-intercooler; verify fuel pressure; test injectors N30–N33

Common_Causes: Leaking injector; high fuel pressure; failed lambda probe; crankcase ventilation fault
Symptoms: Fuel smell; black smoke; fouled spark plugs; rich idle
Recommended_Fix: Check fuel pressure regulating valve N276; test injectors for leakback; inspect crankcase ventilation system

Affected_Component: Not applicable (TSI uses double injection for cat heating instead)
Common_Causes: N/A – The 1.4 TSI uses double injection catalytic converter heating mode rather than secondary air injection
Symptoms: Not applicable
Recommended_Fix: Note: The TSI uses double injection (intake stroke + 50° before TDC) for cat warm-up – secondary air injection is not fitted

Common_Causes: CAN communication fault; ABS sensor fault; J533 data bus diagnostic interface issue
Symptoms: Cruise control disabled; speed-dependent idle control affected
Recommended_Fix: Check CAN bus communication between J623 and J533; verify ABS sensors

Common_Causes: Weak battery; failing alternator; corroded battery terminals; high current draw
Symptoms: Multiple sensor faults triggered simultaneously; boost control erratic; ignition coil energy reduced
Recommended_Fix: Check battery state of charge and health; check alternator output; inspect main earth connections

Common_Causes: Voltage spike; failed ECU; water ingress into ECU (mounted in plenum chamber)
Symptoms: Engine may not start; multiple faults stored simultaneously
Recommended_Fix: Check ECU location in plenum chamber for water ingress; check supply voltage; replace/recode J623

Affected_Component: Throttle Valve Drive G186 (in module J338)
Common_Causes: Failed throttle valve motor; gearbox mechanism binding; wiring fault to G186
Symptoms: Engine enters emergency mode; engine speed limited to 1500rpm; cruise control disabled
Recommended_Fix: Test G186 motor and gearbox; replace J338 throttle valve module

Common_Causes: Failed relay J271; blown fuse; wiring open circuit
Symptoms: Engine will not start; all sensors and actuators fed by J271 lose power
Recommended_Fix: Inspect J271 relay in E-box left of engine compartment; check associated fuses; check wiring

Affected_Component: Throttle Valve Module J338 / Angle Senders G187 and G188
Common_Causes: Both G187 and G188 angle senders failed simultaneously; throttle valve stuck
Symptoms: Engine speed limited to 1500rpm; throttle valve drive switched off; no accelerator response
Recommended_Fix: Replace J338 throttle valve module assembly; check wiring to both G187 and G188

Description: Idle Speed Control Throttle Position Adaptation Outside Limits
Common_Causes: Carbon build-up on throttle valve; throttle valve not returning to base position; failed throttle motor
Symptoms: Poor idle quality; unstable idle; throttle response issues
Recommended_Fix: Clean throttle valve body; perform basic throttle setting adaptation; replace J338 if motor faulty

Affected_Component: Throttle Valve Module J338 / Engine Mechanical
Common_Causes: Significant air leak; throttle body carbon build-up; worn engine components
Symptoms: Poor idle; ECU at limits of idle correction
Recommended_Fix: Check for intake air leaks; clean throttle body

Affected_Component: Engine Control Unit J623 Power Supply
Common_Causes: Low battery voltage during key-on; corroded battery terminal; high resistance in main supply
Symptoms: Multiple simultaneous faults; ECU resets; boost control issues
Recommended_Fix: Check battery condition; check terminal 30 supply fuse and wiring to ECU

Description: Boost Pressure Regulation: Regulating Flap Control Circuit
Affected_Component: Regulating Flap Control Unit J808 / Control Motor V380
Common_Causes: Failed V380 motor; wiring fault; J808 unit defective; regulating flap mechanically stuck
Symptoms: Regulating flap defaults to fully open; supercharger cannot be activated; no boost below 3500rpm
Recommended_Fix: Check V380 motor operation; test regulating flap for mechanical freedom; replace J808 unit if motor confirmed faulty

Common_Causes: Failed G584 potentiometer; wiring fault; regulating flap position signal implausible
Symptoms: Supercharger boost pressure regulation disabled; regulating flap held fully open; engine loses low-rpm performance
Recommended_Fix: Test G584 potentiometer signal sweep; check wiring; replace J808/G584 assembly

Description: Regulating Flap Potentiometer G584 Supply Circuit
Common_Causes: Open circuit in supply or ground to G584; failed J808 unit
Symptoms: No flap position signal; supercharger disabled
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring supply and ground to J808; replace unit if wiring is intact

Description: Supercharger Magnetic Clutch N421: Short Circuit to Ground
Common_Causes: Short to ground in N421 coil wiring; failed N421 solenoid coil; wiring chafed against bodywork
Symptoms: Supercharger continuously engaged or not engageable; clicking noise at clutch
Recommended_Fix: Inspect N421 wiring for short; replace N421 magnetic clutch

Description: Supercharger Magnetic Clutch N421: Open Circuit
Common_Causes: Open circuit in N421 coil wiring; blown fuse; failed solenoid; failed G582 current sensor
Symptoms: Supercharger not activatable; no boost from supercharger below 3500rpm; clicking may stop
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring from J271 relay through G582 to N421; check G582 sensor for open circuit

Description: Sensor for Current Measurement G582: Signal Implausible
Common_Causes: Failed G582 sensor (located in E-box left of engine); wiring fault; diode in sensor failed
Symptoms: Magnetic clutch engages with a jolt instead of gently; if complete failure – supercharger cannot be activated
Recommended_Fix: Locate G582 in left E-box; test sensor output voltage; replace G582

Description: Torque Converter Clutch Solenoid Circuit Electrical
Common_Causes: DSG mechatronic fault; CAN communication fault between J623 and J743
Symptoms: Gear change issues; supercharger magnetic clutch always uses PWM (DSG vehicles); limp mode possible
Recommended_Fix: Check CAN bus; refer to DSG system – fault handled by J743 mechatronic unit

Affected_Component: Fuel Gauge Sender G / Fuel Pump Unit
Common_Causes: Failed sender in fuel supply unit (integrated with G6); wiring fault
Symptoms: Incorrect fuel level reading; possible fuel pump operation issues
Recommended_Fix: Check fuel pump unit assembly including G sender; check wiring

Affected_Component: Fuel Pump Control Unit J538 / Fuel Pump G6
Common_Causes: Failed J538 control unit; open circuit to G6; blown fuse; failed relay
Symptoms: Engine will not run; no fuel delivery
Recommended_Fix: Check J538 location under rear bench; check PWM signal from J623 to J538; check fuse for fuel pump

Common_Causes: Failed fuel pump G6; wiring fault; corroded connector in fuel tank
Symptoms: Engine cranks but will not start; fuel rail does not pressurise
Recommended_Fix: Check fuel pump supply voltage and ground; replace G6 fuel pump unit

Description: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor Circuit Malfunction
Affected_Component: Duo-Centric Oil Pump / Oil Pressure Switch
Common_Causes: Failed oil pressure sender; low oil level; oil pump wear; blocked oil strainer
Symptoms: Oil warning light; potential engine damage if oil pressure genuinely low
Recommended_Fix: Check oil level first; verify oil pressure mechanically; inspect duo-centric oil pump regulation

Common_Causes: Oil pump regulation circuit fault; control ring sticking; incorrect oil viscosity
Symptoms: Oil pressure inconsistent across rev range; oil pressure warning at idle
Recommended_Fix: Check oil viscosity specification; inspect oil pump control spring and control ring; replace pump if regulation fails

Affected_Component: Additional Coolant Pump Relay J496 / Coolant Circulation Pump V50
Common_Causes: Failed J496 relay (left under dash panel); failed V50 pump; wiring fault
Symptoms: Turbocharger overheating after engine off (steam bubbles risk); coolant not circulated post-shutdown for 15 minutes
Recommended_Fix: Check J496 relay location under left dash panel; test V50 pump directly; check wiring

Common_Causes: Failed V50 pump motor; wiring fault; V50 failure not detected by self-diagnosis
Symptoms: Post-shutdown turbocharger overheating; coolant boiling risk in turbocharger
Recommended_Fix: Note: SSP 359 states faulty V50 is NOT recognised by self-diagnosis – suspect V50 if turbo overheating occurs without stored fault

Description: Evaporative Emission Control System Purge Control Valve Circuit
Affected_Component: Active Charcoal Filter System Solenoid Valve N80
Common_Causes: Failed N80 solenoid; wiring open circuit; power supply interrupted
Symptoms: Tank not ventilated; possible fuel smell; EVAP system non-functional
Recommended_Fix: Check N80 location near throttle valve module; verify supply voltage; replace N80 solenoid

Description: Evaporative Emission Control System Purge Control Valve Circuit Open
Affected_Component: Active Charcoal Filter System Solenoid Valve N80
Common_Causes: Open circuit in N80 supply or ground wire
Symptoms: Fuel vapours not purged; tank ventilation failure; fuel odour
Recommended_Fix: Check wiring continuity to N80; replace N80 if wiring is intact

Description: Evaporative Emission Control System Vent Control Circuit Open
Affected_Component: Active Charcoal Filter System / Check Valve
Common_Causes: Check valve failed closed; N80 wiring fault; EVAP hose blocked
Symptoms: Fuel tank pressurisation; fuel smell
Recommended_Fix: Inspect check valve between N80 and turbocharger inlet; check hose routing

Common_Causes: Short to ground in N30 wiring; failed injector solenoid
Symptoms: Cylinder 1 disabled; misfire; rough running
Recommended_Fix: Check N30 wiring for short; replace injector N30 (note: 150 bar max injection pressure on TSI)

Common_Causes: Short to ground in N31 wiring; failed injector solenoid
Symptoms: Cylinder 2 disabled; misfire; rough running
Recommended_Fix: Check N31 wiring for short; replace injector N31

Common_Causes: Short to ground in N32 wiring; failed injector solenoid
Symptoms: Cylinder 3 disabled; misfire; rough running
Recommended_Fix: Check N32 wiring for short; replace injector N32

Common_Causes: Short to ground in N33 wiring; failed injector solenoid
Symptoms: Cylinder 4 disabled; misfire; rough running
Recommended_Fix: Check N33 wiring for short; replace injector N33

Affected_Component: Hall Sender G40 (monitors inlet camshaft only on this engine)
Common_Causes: Note: Only inlet camshaft is adjusted on 1.4 TSI. Outlet camshaft is fixed. G40 monitors inlet only. Fault may indicate G40 or timing chain issue
Symptoms: Timing faults; potential chain-related noise
Recommended_Fix: Verify this is an inlet camshaft fault; check timing chain for wear

Common_Causes: Misfires causing catalyst damage (especially with double-injection heating mode); oil consumption fouling catalyst; exhaust leak at air-gap insulated connecting pipe
Symptoms: MIL on; emissions failure; reduced catalyst efficiency reading from G130
Recommended_Fix: Check exhaust connecting pipe (air-gap insulated) between turbocharger and catalyst for leaks; verify no misfires; replace catalyst if confirmed

Description: Lambda Probe G39 Correction Value at Limit
Common_Causes: Large air leak; failing probe; significant injector imbalance; crankcase ventilation fault
Symptoms: Lambda correction at limit of adaptive range; emissions failure; MIL illuminated
Recommended_Fix: Check all intake air connections post-air filter; check crankcase ventilation check valve and hoses; test G39 probe

Common_Causes: Thermostat 1 (opens at 80°C for cylinder head circuit) failed; coolant distributor housing fault
Symptoms: Cylinder head cooling circuit not regulated properly; knocking tendency may increase; combustion chamber temperature management affected
Recommended_Fix: Replace thermostat 1 assembly; note dual-stage design of TSI thermostat requiring correct replacement part
